From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Weighted line used as a vertical reference

This article is about the tool. For the nuclear testing program, see [Operation Plumbbob](/wiki/Operation_Plumbbob "Operation Plumbbob").

"Plumbline" redirects here. For other uses, see [Plumbline (disambiguation)](/wiki/Plumbline_(disambiguation) "Plumbline (disambiguation)").

"Plumbob" redirects here. For the mood indicator, see [The Sims 2 § Gameplay](/wiki/The_Sims_2#Gameplay "The Sims 2").

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Plumb_bob "Special:EditPage/Plumb bob") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Plumb bob"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Plumb+bob%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Plumb+bob%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Plumb+bob%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Plumb+bob%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Plumb+bob%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Plumb+bob%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(November 2023)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9d/Plumb_bob.jpg/250px-Plumb_bob.jpg)](/wiki/File:Plumb_bob.jpg)

A plumb bob

A **plumb bob**, **plumb bob level**, or **plummet**, is a weight, usually with a pointed tip on the bottom, suspended from a string and used as a [vertical direction](/wiki/Vertical_direction "Vertical direction") as a reference line, or **plumb-line**. It is a precursor to the [spirit level](/wiki/Spirit_level "Spirit level") and used to establish a vertical [datum](/wiki/Datum_reference "Datum reference"). It is typically made of stone, wood, or lead, but can also be made of other metals. If it is used for decoration, it may be made of bone or ivory.

The [instrument](/wiki/Measuring_instrument "Measuring instrument") has been used since at least the time of [ancient Egypt](/wiki/Ancient_Egypt "Ancient Egypt")[[1]](#cite_note-Stocks2003-1) to ensure that constructions are "[plumb](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/plumb "wikt:plumb")", or vertical. It is also used in [surveying](/wiki/Surveying "Surveying"), to establish the [nadir](/wiki/Nadir "Nadir") (opposite of [zenith](/wiki/Zenith "Zenith")) with respect to [gravity](/wiki/Gravity_of_Earth "Gravity of Earth") of a point in space. It is used with a variety of instruments (including [levels](/wiki/Level_(instrument) "Level (instrument)"), [theodolites](/wiki/Theodolite "Theodolite"), and [steel tapes](/wiki/Tape_measure "Tape measure")) to set the instrument exactly over a fixed [survey marker](/wiki/Survey_marker "Survey marker") or to transcribe positions onto the ground for placing a marker.[[2]](#cite_note-Brinker1995-2)

## Etymology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Plumb_bob&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Etymology")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/da/Cc%26j-fig23--plumb_rule.png/120px-Cc%26j-fig23--plumb_rule.png)](/wiki/File:Cc%26j-fig23--plumb_rule.png)

A plumb rule from *[Cassells' Carpentry and Joinery](/w/index.php?title=Cassells%27_Carpentry_and_Joinery&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cassells' Carpentry and Joinery (page does not exist)")*

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/7b/Cc%26j-fig24--plumb_square.png/250px-Cc%26j-fig24--plumb_square.png)](/wiki/File:Cc%26j-fig24--plumb_square.png)

A plumb square from *Cassells' Carpentry and Joinery*

The *plumb* in *plumb bob* derives from Latin *[plumbum](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/plumbum "wikt:plumbum")* ('[lead](/wiki/Lead "Lead")'), the material once used for the weighted *[bob](/wiki/Bob_(physics) "Bob (physics)")* at the end.[[3]](#cite_note-McGeough-3) The adjective *[plumb](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/plumb "wikt:plumb")* developed by extension, as did the noun *[aplomb](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/aplomb "wikt:aplomb")*, from the notion of "standing upright".

## Use

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Plumb_bob&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Use")]

Until the modern age, plumb bobs were used on most tall structures to provide vertical [datum](/wiki/Datum "Datum") lines for the building measurements. A section of the scaffolding would hold a plumb line, which was centered over a datum mark on the floor. As the building proceeded upward, the plumb line would also be taken higher, still centered on the datum. Many cathedral [spires](/wiki/Spire "Spire"), [domes](/wiki/Domes "Domes") and [towers](/wiki/Tower "Tower") still have brass datum marks inlaid into their floors, which signify the center of the structure above.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/da/Lot_hg.jpg/250px-Lot_hg.jpg)](/wiki/File:Lot_hg.jpg)

Plumb bob with scale as an inclinometer

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/dc/Plumb_bob_with_string.jpg/250px-Plumb_bob_with_string.jpg)](/wiki/File:Plumb_bob_with_string.jpg)

Plumb bob with string in masonry work

A plumb bob and line alone can determine only a vertical reference. However, if they are mounted on a suitable scale the instrument may also be used as an [inclinometer](/wiki/Inclinometer "Inclinometer") to measure angles to the vertical.

[Ancient Egyptians](/wiki/Ancient_Egypt "Ancient Egypt") used a plumb line attached to the top outer part of a tool resembling a letter *[E](/wiki/E "E")*; when placed against a wall, the plumb line would indicate a vertical line. An [archipendulum](/wiki/Archipendulum "Archipendulum"), an [A-frame](/wiki/A-frame "A-frame") level with a plumb line hung from the vertex was also used to find horizontal; these were used in Europe until the mid–19th century. A variation of this tool has the plumb line hung from the top of an inverted *[T](/wiki/T "T")* shape.[[3]](#cite_note-McGeough-3)

The early [skyscrapers](/wiki/Skyscraper "Skyscraper") used heavy plumb bobs, hung on wire in their elevator shafts.[*[further explanation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Please_clarify "Wikipedia:Please clarify")*]

A plumb bob may be in a container of water (when conditions are above freezing temperatures), molasses, very viscous oils or other liquids to dampen any swinging movement,[[4]](#cite_note-4) functioning as a [shock absorber](/wiki/Shock_absorber "Shock absorber").

### Determining center of gravity of an irregular shape

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Plumb_bob&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Determining center of gravity of an irregular shape")]

Students of [figure drawing](/wiki/Figure_drawing "Figure drawing") will also make use of a plumb line to find the vertical axis through the [center of gravity](/wiki/Center_of_gravity "Center of gravity") of their subject and lay it down on paper as a [point of reference](/wiki/Frame_of_reference "Frame of reference"). The device used may be purpose-made plumb lines, or simply makeshift devices made from a piece of string and a weighted object, such as a [metal washer](/wiki/Washer_(hardware) "Washer (hardware)"). This plumb line is important for lining up anatomical geometries and visualizing the subject's [center of balance](/wiki/Center_of_mass "Center of mass").

## In popular culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Plumb_bob&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: In popular culture")]

Following the release of *[The Sims](/wiki/The_Sims_(video_game) "The Sims (video game)")* in 2000, a green plumb bob has been used as a [logo](/wiki/Logo "Logo"). In game, the plumb bob to identify the character selected and their moods.[[5]](#cite_note-5) In various expansion packs, bands are added around the plumb bob to represent different life states, such as [vampires](/wiki/Vampire "Vampire"), [mermaids](/wiki/Mermaid "Mermaid"), and [aliens](/wiki/Extraterrestrial_life "Extraterrestrial life").

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Plumb_bob&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: See also")]

## References
